source: Original plain-English summaries by the project author. MIT-licensed creative work. Code documents (NEC, IPC, etc.) referenced by section number only; no code text reproduced.

kitchen.gfci: Required for receptacles serving countertop surfaces and within 6 ft of a sink.
kitchen.afci: Required for branch circuits supplying outlets in dwelling-unit kitchens.

bathroom.gfci: Required for all 125 V single-phase 15- and 20-amp receptacles.

garage.gfci: Required for receptacles installed in garages and accessory buildings.
garage.afci: Not generally required outside dwelling-unit habitable rooms.

outdoor.gfci: Required for all 125 V receptacles in outdoor locations.

bedroom.gfci: Not generally required (unless near a sink).
bedroom.afci: Required for all 120 V branch circuits supplying outlets and devices in dwelling-unit bedrooms.

laundry.gfci: Required for receptacles in laundry areas.
laundry.afci: Required for branch circuits supplying laundry-area outlets.

notes: AHJ governs. The summaries paraphrase widely-known requirements and are not a substitute for the adopted code edition.
